1→---
     2→title: "Introduction - Personal Advice"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4429-4430"
     6→---
     7→
     8→I feel that those who consider these advices will go a long way in their corporate lives. These advices are entirely personal and therefore I leave it to your wisdom whether to consider or not.
     9→

1→---
     2→title: "ATTITUDE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4431-4441"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**ATTITUDE**
     9→
    10→Assume A is designated as 1, B as 2, C as 3 and so on till Z as 26\.  
    11→Now add the numbers against each character of ATTITUDE, ie. 1(A)+20(T)+20(T)+9(I)+20(T)+21(U)+4(D)+5(E) \= 100
    12→
    13→Probably ATTITUDE is the only English word whose designated numeric numbers add to 100\. This is just a coincidence, however the number 100 attached to the word ATTITUDE signifies immense importance.
    14→
    15→You will observe that some people may have the right vision, knowledge and skills but lacks attitude to accomplish a certain work, be it for any reason. The right attitude steers your knowledge and skills into action thereby accomplishing the work in hand. If you do not have the right attitude, you cannot succeed in life however knowledgeable you may be and however great skills you may possess. 
    16→
    17→**Have the correct attitude always; it will pay rich dividends in your life.**
    18→

1→---
     2→title: "BE HUMANE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4442-4445"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**BE HUMANE**
     9→
    10→When a child grows up, relatives and neighbours often ask what you would like to be when you grow up. Some say doctor, some say engineer, and some even say pilot. But I taught my child, which I suppose every parent should, that when asked about his future, he should say that he wants to be a good human being apart from anything else that someone wants to be. Being a good human being goes a long way in your life, even in the corporate world as you may have heard about Sir Ratan Tata. Good human beings can manage and deal with people better in the corporate. This is an advantage to them apart from the vision, knowledge and skills they possess.
    11→

1→---
     2→title: "PEOPLE MANAGEMENT"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4446-4458"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**PEOPLE MANAGEMENT**
     9→
    10→I still remember one of the wise words that my boss taught when I stepped into the Marketing domain in my earlier career. “Listen Abhik, while you build your career in Marketing, you will come across various kind of people; good and bad, honest and dishonest, polite and angry, knowledgeable and not knowledgeable, helpful and not so helpful, co-operative and jealous, kind and unkind, biased and unbiased, etc., so you must (1) learn how to deal with all these kind of people because you cannot simply ignore their presence in the organization and (2) learn how to say “No’ with a smile because you cannot say yes to proposals and statements that you feel are not right.
    11→
    12→In fact when I assumed the position of Marketing Head and stayed on for ten years and eventually became the Business Head and stayed on for the last three years of my career, I found that managing people was the most difficult job for me. As an organization, I encountered many technical and financial challenges but I had the support of expertise in all the fields of operation, be it in Designs, Projects, Supply Chain, Quality, Safety or Finance or any other department whatsoever to overcome these challenges. But Heads and senior people often had different opinions, sometimes judgmental, contradictory and sometimes steered by vested interest, jealousy and anger. So hearing out everyone, using my vision and knowledge and taking a final decision was often very challenging because the final decision did not adhere to some of the opinions yet I had to convince all as to why the final decision has been taken.   
    13→Every human being has an ego which they wish to be satisfied. You cannot ignore people who work with you, yet you need to take decisions which are not in line with some of the opinions. 
    14→
    15→People are very sensitive. All their opinions must be heard to. At times, their opinions may be disregarded and at times theirs could be the best option. You have to treat all your colleagues humanly with due respect. And you must have the right attitude and power to convince all the reasons for your final decision.  
    16→   
    17→Hence I found man management the most difficult job in my career as well as the most interesting and challenging. 
    18→
    19→**Develop man management skills to succeed in life.**
    20→

1→---
     2→title: "FIRST OPPORTUNITY"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4459-4470"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**FIRST OPPORTUNITY**
     9→
    10→Opportunity comes and goes. Sometimes it comes only once. You should grab opportunity at the first time itself, provided it’s reasonably good, lest a better one does not come later. You have to hold your greed and grab a reasonable opportunity at the first instance itself because most of the times a better opportunity does not come again.
    11→
    12→Once in my early part of my career, I was selected for a management course at the Imperial Institute of Management in UK by the company. I was reluctant because I was comfortable in my job at Kolkata and I thought to undergo the course after a year or two and till then to earn some money. I due course of time, the sponsorship was stopped by the company and that opportunity never came again. Eventually, much later on, I had to settle for a Advanced Diploma Course in Management from the Institute of Management Technology (IIMT) through a distance learning mode. 
    13→
    14→Once in a Maruti True Value showroom, when I did not have enough money to buy a new car, I spotted a car which I wished to buy since the price was very reasonable. I kept it on my buy list. In greed to get a better deal, I visited another showroom but could not get a deal as good as the earlier one. I rushed to the first showroom again to seal the deal but the car was sold by then. Eventually I purchased a car but at a higher price.
    15→
    16→Most of the times I have observed that the first opportunity I got were the best opportunity. And in case the second opportunity was better, leave the first one and take the second if possible and if not possible then don’t repent.
    17→
    18→**Grab the first opportunity rather than be greedy to grab a better one later**.
    19→

1→---
     2→title: "INSURANCE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4471-4478"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**INSURANCE**
     9→
    10→Insurance is a cover for an individual to safeguard his interest. For example life insurance will safeguard against the livelihood costs of the nominee and his family in case of death, health insurance will safeguard against the medical costs for treatment, property insurance will safeguard against EMI payments by the nominee in case of death, house insurance will safeguard against the costs for damage of house due to calamities, cyber insurance will safeguard against cyber frauds and threats.
    11→
    12→You have to pay premiums for taking such insurances. But many wise people argue foolishly at the end of a year that they have been profitable by not taking insurance cover and thus paying premiums since nothing happened to them in the year. Financially it may be so but technically not. No one desires that something bad happens to them ever; however, one should think that you need to de-risk such undesirable events. Hence is the importance of insurance. All individuals are advised to take insurances. 
    13→
    14→**Insurance is a cover for risk; the premium is for attaining peace of mind.**
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "HEALTH"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4479-4502"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**HEALTH**
     9→
    10→Health is wealth. We understand it better as we age and we sometimes find ourselves in unhealthy conditions or become sick. While we are young, being sick is rare and so we then think Money is Wealth, not Health. However, today many youngsters are prone to sickness due to many factors, personal and environmental.
    11→
    12→As we remain busy in our corporate life, health, the most precious asset that one possess, is often ignored. Continuous such ignorance may lead to health complications later on. One must sincerely take care of his health while on service. Do not postpone it till retirement. Maintain health right from day one.
    13→
    14→Health pertains to different domains of an individual;  
    15→Physical health is about taking care of your body;  
    16→Mental health is about taking care of your mind;  
    17→Spiritual health is about taking care of your soul;
    18→
    19→Body, mind and soul are all interrelated and define your wellbeing. Your satisfactory wellbeing is a catalyst to a good personal or corporate life. If your body or the mind or the soul or any combination thereof is disturbed, your personal life and corporate life too will be disturbed.
    20→
    21→Health pertaining to other domains of an individual is:  
    22→Financial health is about managing your finances and assets  
    23→Sexual health is about taking interest in your sex life  
    24→Social health is about your involvement in the society  
    25→Job health is about your satisfaction in your job or business
    26→
    27→Above health parameters too affect your wellbeing. For example, if your financial health is poor, you will be anxious and will hamper your mental health. And if your mental health is poor, your physical health will be affected. All are interrelated.
    28→
    29→Also, don’t miss your annual or bi-annual basic health check-ups comprising of blood tests, Chest x-ray, USG and ECG. It is interesting to note that many people think they are fine and healthy but health check-ups reveal that certain health parameters are beyond normal values which require immediate treatment. What I am trying to sensitise that many such illness develop within our bodies SILENTLY.  For example, thousands of people do not know that they are pre-diabetic or have high blood pressure because they don’t check. These are silent killers. So please don’t miss your health check-ups.    
    30→**Health is wealth. It is one of the most precious assets that one possesses. Please don’t ignore**
    31→

1→---
     2→title: "PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4503-4520"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT**
     9→
    10→This is again a ignored subject while we are in service. Someone comes and convinces of investing in a FD, we do it. Someone comes and asks to invest in ULIPs, we do it. Someone else comes and convinces us that there is lot of money to be earned in the stock market, we invest in the equities. Someone says to buy a flat instead of paying rents, we do so. That someone could be you yourself. You may think to put some money in gold, you do it. Whatever someone tries to convince or we are convinced about investment, we do it. However, all these investments are done in a haphazard manner.
    11→
    12→Here comes the issue of portfolio management. There is no harm in investing money in different class of assets, but it has to be done in a scientific manner. Firstly, the class of assets can be classified into equity, debt, property and metal. The percentage of investment into these different class could be based on one's age (the less the age, the more may be invested in equities), risk taking power (the less the risk taking power is, the more may be invested in debts), interest (if on a transferable job, someone may not be interested in property). 
    13→
    14→A typical mid aged person with moderate risk taking power can invest like;  
    15→35% on equity  
    16→25% on debt  
    17→25% on property  
    18→15% on metal
    19→
    20→Equity could be the stock market or equity MFs. Debt could be bank FD, corporate FD and corporate bonds. Property could be residential or commercial. Metal could be physical gold or silver, paper gold or silver, platinum or copper, zinc and aluminum in the MCX stock exchange. 
    21→
    22→Please take the help of a portfolio manager, who could be your Finance guy himself, to guide you for a good portfolio.
    23→
    24→**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**
    25→

1→---
     2→title: "WORK-LIFE BALANCE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4521-4530"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**WORK-LIFE BALANCE**
     9→
    10→This is a hot debate often happening even on TV debates. How many live s have God given you? One, I suppose. Therefore you are never going to get the time lapsed of your life. Even the richest man on earth cannot buy time which has lapsed. Time gone is gone forever. And your past is also gone forever.
    11→
    12→So, while we have to struggle very hard in the corporates to do well in life and rise up the corporate ladder, we also need to give time for yourself (me time) and your family and friends. All is not work in life. We definitely have to work; it is a foundation to have a great life with our family and friends. So you need to balance between work and life and that is what is called the Work-Life balance. If you provide all the time to work, your relationship with your family may not be so intimate and loving, you may lose your friends and you may end up leaving all your hobbies that you so much loved to pursue.
    13→
    14→For a few days the balance may be upset due to pressure of work or on family matters, but on an overall basis you must strike a balance between work and life.
    15→
    16→**WORK-LIFE balance is about providing time to office as well as to our near and dear ones.**
    17→

1→---
     2→title: "GRATEFULNESS"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4531-4546"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**GRATEFULNESS**
     9→
    10→Why do we touch the feet of our parents when we meet them? Why do we touch the feet of our beloved school teachers or our coaches? Because we are grateful to them for the care, love and teachings they have imparted to us. Without their care and support, we would not have been what we are; therefore they have been large contributors to our success in life.
    11→
    12→However, very unfortunately, I have seen many people not being grateful to their contributors once the contributor is no more in a position to contribute to you anymore. For example, as a business Head, you might have taught the intricacies of business to your subordinate and encouraged him and promoted him to assume higher responsibilities which he was capable of, but as you retire and you are no more in a position to give good bonus or promote him any further you are simply forgotten. It is often said that in a street or a market a clerk or a staff do not even look at a Director who have retired from his organization because the clerk knows that the Director now cannot give him any benefits further. These people are so selfish because they think why they should waste time, energy and money to talk or meet their bosses when they are useless for them\!  
    13→
    14→Above kind of incidences happen in every sphere of life, even within our relatives and “so called” friends. So why should someone be grateful. It has been proven scientifically they gratefulness leads to:
    15→
    16→1) Emotional well-being: Gratitude fosters positive emotions, reduces stress. Expressing appreciation leads to increased happiness and satisfaction of the soul  
    17→2) Physical well-being: Gratitude improves physical health by promoting better sleep, reduces blood pressure and enhances immunity  
    18→3) Mental well-being: Gratitude lowers depression and anxiety and helps in facing challenges in life  
    19→4) Social connections: Gratitude strengthens relationships and reduces loneliness  
    20→5) Contentment: Gratitude leads to greater contentment. It shifts focus from what one lacks to what one has promoting a sense of fulfillment
    21→
    22→**Practice gratefulness; you may not be aware how it improves your well-being**
    23→

1→---
     2→title: "WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4547-4552"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS**
     9→
    10→I find it frustrating when one tries to figure out only the negative sides of a person, an option or an entity. Be it yourself, or a singer, or an actor, or a politician, or a team, or a political party, or a school, or whatever, there can be good things as well as bad things about it. Any human or agency or entity cannot be perfect in all parameters. But a good human or an agency or an entity has presumably many more good things than the bad things about them. You need to appreciate those good things and not ignore and focus on bad things. You can also weigh the importance of the good things and the bad things and come to a conclusion if the entity is good or bad.
    11→
    12→In our personal life and also in the corporate, we sometimes have many options to select from. You must weigh the positive and negative factors of each of the options and come to a conclusion on which option to choose. It is obvious that all options will have some negative points. There are people who are not happy with any option because he tries to see only the negative side of each option. These people cannot make proper and fast decisions needed in the corporate world. You have to choose an option with the greatest advantages amidst some disadvantages that it may have.
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "SABJANTALOGY"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4553-4564"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**SABJANTALOGY**
     9→
    10→This is the name of a subject which I coined after coming across some people who fit into this class of people. Just like Biology, Physiology, Oncology, Sociology, Anthropology, etc., Sabjantalogy deals with the science of knowing everything on earth.
    11→
    12→Some people may advise you on how to cook better, advise you on better medicines, teach you how to play a sports better, seemingly knows about any kind of industry on earth, knows about fabric and garments, gives you lecture on politics, or even on rocket science. The most astonishing part is that all these “knowhow??” has been formed on his own whims and fancies and not formed by reading books or learning from somewhere.
    13→
    14→Say for example, if you are an Arts graduate and have formed an idea about a medicine with your own experiment without consulting a doctor or learning it from any medical journal or reading about it from newspaper or elsewhere, is it right for you to prescribe the medicine to your friend? There can be hundreds of examples like this on various subjects.
    15→
    16→Today the world has lot of expertise in every field of life. If you really want to prescribe a medicine, ask the doctor, or if you really want to advise on how to make the best cakes, ten consult youtube or a chef. Why provide your “so called” knowledge which is actually wrong.
    17→
    18→**Don’t be a Sabtanjalogist; ask the specialist or learn about it.**
    19→

1→---
     2→title: "DON’T BE A MISER"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4565-4574"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**DON’T BE A MISER**
     9→
    10→What is the purpose of earning money; to spend it wisely and reasonably; Isn’t it? But some accumulate money for their own satisfaction and not spend them for reasons not known to them. They are misers. Misers are of two types: universal misers and selfish misers. Universal misers are those who are reluctant to spend for anyone on earth. Selfish misers are those who are willing to spend only for him and family but not beyond.
    11→
    12→Spending must be wise and reasonable; you need to keep reasonable amount of money for medical emergencies, education and marriage of your children and for all mandatory purposes of life. Beyond that, you should spend for yourself, family and others for things that you love in life, be it for food, movies, clothes, gadget, tours and travels, hobbies or whatever that you think will be useful or make someone happy. Remember, there is no concept of Fixed Deposits in heaven. Everything that you earn in life has to be left. We have been born naked and will depart to Heaven naked. It is one life that God has given us. Enjoy it to the fullest extent. 
    13→
    14→How much to save, how much to invest and how much to spend may vary between individuals, but they have to be wisely and reasonably thought of practically. Allot money that you earn for savings, investment and expenses in a balanced practical manner.
    15→
    16→**Save & invest what is necessary. But spend too to enjoy and live your life.**
    17→

1→---
     2→title: "SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4575-4582"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD**
     9→
    10→There is a joke which you will hear while your child grows up. When you are asked how tall your child has grown up, you answer by gesture with your hands apart horizontally. This is because your child leaves for school early while you are asleep and the child is asleep when you come home late from office. Therefore you have not seen him grow tall vertically on office days. Suddenly you can see him how tall he is when you are at home on Sundays. 
    11→
    12→However, do not take it as a simple joke. Once your child is 18 years old and pass out Class XII, he may be going out of station or abroad for higher studies and subsequently for good jobs anywhere on this earth. This means you may get to stay with your child at your home only for the first 18 years of his life and thereafter you will miss him except for a few days when the child will have time to meet you at home on his holidays.
    13→
    14→At the same time, you will be very busy at office trying to secure your good future year after year. But amidst this rat race, do not ignore to spend as much time as possible with your child. Remember you may not get to see him for long after he reaches 18 years of age. Your good times spent with your child will remain etched in your mind forever. Do not miss these times. It will never come back again
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "CULTIVATE A HOBBY"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4583-4588"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**CULTIVATE A HOBBY**
     9→
    10→There will be times in your life when you are very sad. Maybe your boss did not give you a promotion which you thought of sure to get, or could be your fiancé decided for a break up, or incidents like these. You would not like to be besides of your friends or family at such sad moments. You would prefer to be alone for some while. So how do you spend these times alone? Cultivate a hobby which will keep you happy and busy to tide over your sad times or after your retirement. You can learn to play cards on your own; you can learn to play an instrument; you can learn to sing; you can learn to paint; or any other hobby that will give you a sense of fulfillment.
    11→
    12→Music was my greatest de-stressor. Playing the harmonica and the guitar lit up my soul at sad times. These instruments are still a part of my life after retirement.
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4589-4596"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM**
     9→
    10→I have read it everywhere that even a peon would not like to wish or see your face on the street after you have retired as a Director or from a big position in the corporate. This may be true but not always. 
    11→
    12→As a leader, if you had shown good leadership by demonstrating correct attitude, being kind and supportive, managing people humanly with care and respect, being grateful to people who supported you, encouraging people to do well and rewarding them for good work and your actions on employees and all stakeholders always being unbiased and transparent and to top it all if you had been successful in your organization, then people, whoever knew you and know you presently hearing from others do remember and respect you even after you retire or even die. 
    13→
    14→Being unbiased even with your close colleagues and demonstrating your professionalism against personal bias at all times is seen as a great leadership. Such successful leaders are never forgotten.
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "ENJOY LIFE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4597-4608"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**ENJOY LIFE**
     9→
    10→The Almighty have given you ONE life only. Even if your super rich, you have one life only. So always enjoy your life.
    11→
    12→How many hours do you have in a year? 24 hours a day x365 days a year \= 8760 hours. Assume you sleep 8 hours a day; therefore you spend 8 x 365 hours \= 2920 hours. Assume you work 10 hours a day (except Sundays and 12 annual holidays) and commute 2 hours a day to office; therefore you spend 12 x (365-52-12) hours \= 3612 hours. So you have (8760-2920-3612) hours, ie. 2228 hours a year excluding time for work and sleep. 2228 hours out of 8760 hours means around 25% of your life; could be less if you work more. Have you ever imagined that you have only 25% of your life free of work and sleep which you can utilize for yourself, family and friends and relatives? If you consider 5% time gone for maintaining your house, household works, taking your child to school and tuitions, purchase of daily groceries, etc. then you are left with 20% of your life time only. Sounds pretty less\! But that is the fact of life. So enjoy your life well with this 20% of your time. It shall also rejuvenate you to carry on with your work at office. You can do so by allotting time for:
    13→
    14→1) Savouring good food at home and restaurants  
    15→2) Entertainment like hobbies, movies, fairs, gossiping with friends, etc  
    16→3) Tours and Travels – local, out of station and international destinations
    17→
    18→Sorry, if I have missed anything that you would like to enjoy life it. Whatever it is, remember what Mahatma Gandhi said \- “Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever”
    19→

1→---
     2→title: "SEEING THROUGH DISRUPTORS"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4609-4622"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**SEEING THROUGH DISRUPTORS**
     9→
    10→Today you will come across many disrupters during your personal and corporate life. 
    11→
    12→**Technological disruptors** will force you to change methods and techniques in doing your work and execute your project. As a simple example, when computers came in, typists were forced to learn software like Word, Excel, etc. to type on computers since the type writers were abolished. Any typist who did not do so lost his job.
    13→
    14→**Geo-political disruptors** will force you to change the way you do business. As an example the tariff episodes between USA and many other countries including India is forcing many countries to look into other avenues for their business. Such disruptors play havoc on the stock markets with their volatility.
    15→
    16→**Social disruptors** like break up of marriage, divorce, suicide, crimes, etc. will disturb your peace of mind which can affect your personal and corporate life. 
    17→
    18→**Environmental disruptors** like pollution, food adulteration, climate change, etc. is taking a toll in our health.
    19→
    20→In the spate of all these disruptors, you have to see through them with calm and pass them off because most are external inflicted where you have no control. It is easier said than done, but better if it can be done. You need to build a strong mind which is not easy to be disrupted. You must develop capabilities to control your mind and not allow your mind to take control of you.
    21→

1→---
     2→title: "QUIT IF UNHAPPY"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4623-4628"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**QUIT IF UNHAPPY**
     9→
    10→You can work in your company in two ways; one is with stress or pressure that is on you to perform and the other is with passion. 
    11→
    12→When you work with passion, you forget how long you have been working on because you work with interest not by any external force or pressure to perform. When you work for long with stress and pressure, you will undergo hormonal changes in your body harming your health. Try to find a new suitable job where you can work happily before your health deteriorates. I know finding a new job is not easy, but go on trying to save your health. Quit.
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "CONTENTMENT IN LIFE"
     3→part: 29
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4629-4636"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**CONTENTMENT IN LIFE**
     9→
    10→Once a middle aged officer approached me in my cabin and informed that he would like to resign because of his low pay. I was astonished to hear about the low pay and called him along with the HR manager later on. On scrutiny of his pay slip, I found that his take home pay was very less but also observed that there were lot of deductions from his salary. On deeper scrutiny I found that there were several EMIs being deducted from his salary, one for new 3BHK property, one for SUV car, one for a racing bike and another for a 75 inch LED TV. I had to only convey that the company is providing you a reasonable salary but it is due to your heavy purchases that your take home salary is meager. 
    11→
    12→Today many youngsters wants to have a property, car and a bike within latest 30 years of age, forget about other costly gadgets which they term them as necessary. There is no end to a materialistic world today. You must put a full stop to buying at some point of time. If you have a hatchback, you will want to have a sedan, then a SUV, then a Mercedes or a BMW, then a Ferrari, where is the end? There is no contentment in your life them, and therefore you will always remain unhappy. You need to balance your pocket. As I said earlier, you have to allot money wisely and reasonably for your savings, investment and your expenses, the total being what comes to your pocket.
    13→
    14→If you develop the Attitude to use money judicially and be happy with whatever you have, you will achieve a state of contentment. Contentment is about being satisfied and happy with whatever you have in life. Contentment does not look at what you do not have in life. **Achieve a state of contentment; you will be happy in life**
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "Introduction - My Learning"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4637-4638"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**PART 30: SOME LESSONS I LEARNT**
     9→

1→---
     2→title: "The Tongue"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4639-4648"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**The Tongue**  
     9→What is the most important organ of human life? Obviously the heart which pumps blood more than 100,000 times a day, which means if you live for 75 years, it will pump approx. 2.75 billion times. Most miraculously, it pumps on its own; you don’t do anything for it to pump, except for taking care of it sometimes with good food and aerobic exercise. With the blood being pumped by the heart, all other organs play their vital role in their own domains.
    10→
    11→But what is the most important organ for living a good life? I say “the tongue”. Tongue is the only organ for the sense of taste and unhealthy but tasty food destroys our health. Food is a vital factor of our health other than some other lifestyle habits. So our tongue is responsible for our physical health. 
    12→
    13→Also we can speak because of our tongue. The way we speak determines our character and this character determines your relationship, be it with your family or friends or relatives or neighbours or colleagues in the corporate. The way politicians speak also determines his relationship with the public because politicians are public figures. So tongue is the main organ for your relationships. It is said that deaf and dumb do not have enemies; yes, they may have the tongue but cannot speak. 
    14→
    15→**Lesson**: The tongue is responsible for two major aspects in one’s life; your physical health and your relationships. Watch your tongue from now on.  
    16→\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→

1→---
     2→title: "True Friends"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4649-4656"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**True Friends**  
     9→Today I am living my third life. I was returning from a seminar from an outstation place to my house. All my juniors were also returning by a bus. The seniors were returning in their own cars. The cars were ahead and the bus was following us. My driver was relatively new on the highway. He put the brakes of my car much before; almost 10 feet before a truck suddenly appeared, not realizing that a heavy 50 ton trailer was coming from behind. As soon as the car stopped, the trailer banged my car from behind and it went flying for quite a distance. The windscreen behind was shattered and the broken glass scattered inside the car. There was a spare wheel kept at the back of my car on which the trailer actually banged. Maybe the wheel absorbed most of the impact. Even if the broken glass splintered, I did not get single scratch on my forehead or eyes. I was not even injured. The driver was slightly injured. I was fine physically but shocked with trauma. I went inside the police station which was just besides the accident spot.
    10→
    11→All my colleagues, some them perceived friends, alighted from the bus coming from behind and came to meet me in the police station. Astonishingly I observed that almost all barring three of them consoled me but left for their homes citing important personal works. The three of them were all along with me helping me to talk to the police, file FIR, arrange a towing vehicle to take my car to the workshop and arrange another sedan car to take me home comfortably. **That day I realized who my true friends were**\!
    12→
    13→**Lesson**: You may have many friends flocking around you even in the corporate world. But be careful, most of them are perceived to be friends, only some are true friends. It’s possible to identify true friends at times of distress only.  
    14→\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "Health is Wealth"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4657-4662"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Health is Wealth**  
     9→As I started leading a second life, sometime later in life, I encountered chest pain occasionally. In the beginning I just thought them to be gastric pains and I presumed that the antacids were working. However, on a severe unbearable chest pain one day, I had to be hospitalized. The doctor smiled and was casual; I was annoyed a bit and asked the doctor “I had terrible chest pain and have come to consult you, but it seems you are smiling and taking it casually”. The doctor replied “my dear friend, do you know how lucky you are? Do you know how many patients come here brought dead? At least by the grace of God, you are still living and after necessary interventions you will be all right. That’s why I am smiling with a sigh of relief”. I had to undergo angioplasty on that day. **I started living a healthy life thereafter and believed Health is Wealth**
    10→
    11→**Lesson**: Bad times act as warnings which lead to correct your way of life. Health is Wealth   
    12→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "Positive outlook"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4663-4668"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Positive outlook**  
     9→After angioplasty and taking rest for two weeks, I resumed office. Sometimes worries crept into my mind about the chest pain I had. Some relatives, deriving sadistic pleasure, even commented that these stents last for 10 years (Recall my topic on “Sabjantalogy”\!) On reaching the office, I found that preparation of celebration is on with cake cutting ceremony and a “healthy” lunch”. I asked my boss “what is this nonsense about?” He replied “Look Abhik, you had blockage and have undergone angioplasty. In fact you were lucky to have chest pain and land in the hospital. Now with the stents implanted, the blood is flowing merrily through your arteries. And your life is extended to some decades. But we live anxiously with blockages, as all humans have, and we do not know what fate lies upon us”. I really wondered about his positive thought. It gave me hope and strength to live on happily leaving behind my worries.
    10→
    11→**Lesson**: Live happily with hope; hope provides strength at all times  
    12→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4669-4674"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence**  
     9→I want to share a series of very short stories which made an impact on my life. The first one – you may have observed that a father plays with his small child by throwing him up on the air and again holding him tightly on his fall. As the child is thrown, he giggles with laughter and joy but has no fear of falling. Why? Because he knows for certain that his father is there to hold him for sure while he falls. **This is TRUST**. The second one \- Once on a hot summer afternoon, the village head asked all the villagers to gather at 10 am the next day and pray to rain god to give rains to the palace. Next day all the people, young and old, came under the scorching heat to pray to the rain god. A little young girl came with an umbrella over her head. When asked, she said it will rain after our prayers. **This is HOPE.** The third one – Sometimes when a cricketer goes to a field to bat, he looks up at the sun. Or someone prays to God before he goes to take an examination. I have also seen footballers touching the field with respect before he starts playing for a match. What are all these gestures like looking up at the sun or praying to God or touching the football field? The individuals believe that they will succeed if they resort to such acts. **This is FAITH**. The fourth one – A student studies very well for the IIT exams. He solves all the past papers. He takes coaching classes and scores very well in all the mock tests. He says he is sure to crack the exams. **This is CONFIDENCE**. 
    10→
    11→**Lesson**: Live and work with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence and implement your Knowledge and Skills, you are sure to succeed  
    12→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "Be perseverant"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4675-4682"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Be perseverant**  
     9→When I became the Marketing Head in charge of Business Development, Marketing and Bidding in 2007, I started submitting tenders to various clients. As months passed by, I found that I was losing all the tenders and it’s a tough job. Almost 8 months passed and I lost almost 20 tenders successively. Not a single one I could win. Then one day I was on a tour and I heard that a meeting of the entire office was held by the then Business Head. On my return, I got a call from my colleague and I learnt that I was severely criticized as a Head and for my failures in the last 8 months. Not only this, The Business Head proposed to replace my position with someone better. I was very sad. I did not speak a single word about this in the office. But I wondered what wrong I am doing all these days. In the next tender, I went through the nitty-gritties of the costing, worked out every minute detail with my colleagues and submitted our price bid. The project was big and prestigious. Eventually we became L1 and were awarded the project. My Business Head changed his mind and congratulated me. I still remember my friend saying to me “Abhik, there is light at the end of each tunnel; Never lose hope”. There was no looking back again. Tenders after tenders were won and I became a prominent figure in the industry, recognized and appreciated with jealousy by our competitors too.
    10→
    11→In another instance, for some reason or the other, I was not getting promotion for many years though I was doing good work. Every year after the appraisals, my mother used to ask me “Did you get promotion this time”. “No” was my answer. This answer went on for consecutive years. I still worked hard. I learnt to be perseverant. I consoled myself by understanding that there may not have been enough vacancies for promotion or that some were better than me. Nothing prevented me to carry on with my hard work. I always focused on excelling on every job.  
    12→
    13→**Lesson**: Never lose hope; Try and try; Be perseverant; Focus on your work: You are bound to succeed  
    14→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "Humble & Caring"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4683-4694"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Humble & Caring**  
     9→I heard from my seniors that once in Bombay House, the corporate office of the Tata group, a new liftman was employed. In the very first day of office of the liftman armed with all the rules of using the lift, he turned away a tall gentleman who wanted to use the lift to go to first floor. The rules did not allow this and the gentleman walked up the stairs. All his floe colleagues rushed to him and said “Hey\! Are you mad? Why did you make him go up the stairs? Don’t you know who he is?” The liftman was amazed and said “No, I just followed the rules”. One of the staff then said “You are surely going to lose your job. The gentleman was Sir Ratan Tata, our Chairman”. The liftman almost lost his sense in shock. He was cursing himself. He knew he will be called sometime later and be sacked. He was very sad to have done such a mistake without knowing though he followed the rules. And after sometime, someone came down and conveyed to the liftman that the Chairman have called him. By this time the liftman was half dead. He sobbingly went upstairs and found the Chairman with his entire team assembled in the middle of the hall. He thought he was in the midst of a punishment in public. The Chairman started saying “look gentlemen; here is a man I found doing his duties very obediently and managing people on the lift irrespective of who you are. He sent me walking to this floor and did not allow me to take the lift. I appreciate his duties because he was following all the rules of using the lift”. This very same day the liftman was rewarded. Such was the greatness of Sir Ratan Tata.
    10→
    11→In another instance, I was watching a TV interview with the staff of Infosys. The staff was mentioning that the Chairman, Mr Narayan Murthy sometimes visits the residential block where labour resides and enquires the wellbeing of the people, whether street lights, drinking water and other amenities are in place. Such feelings for the labour staff are highly appreciated of a great person.
    12→
    13→In another instance of our President, Dr Abdul Kalam, who was also the Chairman of DRDO, asked one of his colleagues, who was a work alcoholic, to take his wife and children to a movie in the evening after office. The person was so busy with his work that he realized its already night and repented that he forgot to take his family to the movie. He left office and reached home with guilt on his face. He apologized to his wife for his forgetfulness. But the wife said that they had already seen the movie and come. He was amazed. He exclaimed “How”. The wife said “since you were busy in work and it was time for the movie, Dr Abdul Kalam came to our house himself and took us for the movie”. Can someone imagine the greatness of this gentleman\!
    14→
    15→The moral of these stories is that treat all people, even your juniors, with kindness and consideration. You do not lose your ego by being kind and considerate to your juniors, rather you demonstrate yourself as a great leader and a human being.
    16→
    17→**Lesson**: Be humble; Be caring; That is what a great leader is.  
    18→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→

1→---
     2→title: "Appreciate Performers"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4695-4702"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Appreciate Performers**  
     9→Once I started doing well as the Marketing Head winning sizeable number of bids, my MD wished that the top 10 performers of the company, which included me too, be facilitated by the then Chairman of the Tata Group, Mr Cyrus Mistry. It was a moment in my corporate life to be cherished forever. Mr Mistry came to our HO at Hyderabad and facilitated me and the others with a sincere word of appreciation. I cannot forget ever the gesture of my MD and the Chairman to have done this for me. I remain ever grateful to them. And later in life, I cried when I heard the news of Mr Mistry’s death.
    10→
    11→In another instance, when I was already succeeding as Marketing Head winning bids, my MD came to our office for a meeting with my bosses. The bosses accompanied the MD upstairs to our office and headed for his cabin past my own. When he saw me, he first came inside my cabin along with my bosses and almost fell on my feet.  I was shocked. I said “No No sir, please do not do that”. He said “Abhik, winning bids only allow our company to execute projects and everyone has a job to do in this company only when you win bids, so I am grateful to you and your team”. Tears rolled down my years on seeing his appreciation. I had no words to say.
    12→
    13→**Lesson**: Appreciate performers; Encourage and Reward them  
    14→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "Silence"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4703-4708"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Silence**  
     9→Sometimes when we argue and protest for something, the other person may be adamant, not willing to listen to your logic, and keep on arguing to prove that you are wrong. It happens in the corporate too. Particularly when you argue with your seniors like a senior relative or your boss to put your points in place and the other person does not agree and keep on arguing, you find yourself frustrated wasting unnecessary time. In such times, I have stayed away from such people with no contacts at all for long durations. In the corporate, you can maintain silence to the extent possible except for when you need to discuss or interact. Often I have observed that your silence speaks much louder than words and helps the other person to realize that he was adamant and wrong. I have adopted this policy even with my relatives whenever required.
    10→
    11→**Lesson**: Silence is often the best form of protest  
    12→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→

1→---
     2→title: "Be Respectful"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4709-4716"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Be Respectful**  
     9→Once my mother rebuked me for no wrong I had done. I gave her back. I did not understand the pain I inflicted on her. I loved her very much and I repented that I was harsh and aggressive. I could have replied in a much respectful manner and not insult her, however, sticking to my point of view.
    10→
    11→In a similar manner, I had scolded a colleague very harshly who was much junior in position to me but much senior to me by age. I saw him almost on the verge of collapsing. I learnt one big lesson in life. Even if you have to reprimand someone, particularly higher in age, for something, you can do it in a respectful manner without being harsh. If required, both can share their point of view for better understanding. Even strict punishments can be given as and when necessary, but it has to be in a respectful manner and humanly because older people probably cannot digest insult as easily as they could when they were young. Be polite and respectful to senior people.  That is a sign of good leadership.
    12→
    13→**Lesson**: Be respectful; It is a sign of good leadership  
    14→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→

1→---
     2→title: "Whatever happens, it happens for good"
     3→part: 30
     4→section: 5
     5→original_lines: "4717-4722"
     6→---
     7→
     8→**Whatever happens, it happens for good**  
     9→Look at so many incidences I narrated before. I met with an accident while coming home from a seminar, but the incident revealed who my true friends were. I was hospitalized after a severe chest pain and underwent angioplasty, but the event taught me to live healthier and realize that Health is Wealth. I did not get promotion for long eight years in my early part of my career, but it helped me to be more perseverant and live with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence. When great leaders like the former Tata group Chairman and my MD facilitated me for my success, I learnt what appreciation by humble and caring leaders mean. When seniors accused me wrongly, I learnt how silence can be the best form of protest.
    10→
    11→Even when my father and mother died, I thought that it is for their good that their souls would be relieved from the sufferings and pain they were in. It pained me a lot on their demise, but I believed that the truth of death being certain is a creation of the Almighty for something good. Maybe that helped me to become independent from the shelter of my parents and be able to do tough work for which I was dependent on my parents. Loss of your loved one probably provides you greater strength to live on with your life, though at times the emotions of the loss is immeasurable. 
    12→
    13→In every undesired event, I learnt something new. Maybe that is the positive way to look at undesirable events. I am not here to prove that every undesirable event will lead to learning something new. But believing in “whatever happens, it happens for good” provides strength to live on with hope of something good lies ahead, triggers new thought process and new learning and helps to tide over such bad or sad uneventful times.
    14→
